atoms.semantic_claim: The crate declares an explicit empty default feature set.

destinations.snippet:
[dependencies]
reth-consensus.workspace = true
reth-execution-errors.workspace = true
reth-storage-errors.workspace = true
destinations.evidence: The destination manifest omits a [features] section entirely; Cargo already defaults to no enabled features when no default feature is declared, so `default = []` adds no behavior by itself.

atoms.equivalence_argument: In Cargo, absence of a `default` feature declaration already means no default features are enabled. This line is only scaffolding for the real passthrough feature and is behaviorally redundant on its own.
atoms.notes: Treat as redundant manifest noise rather than a standalone porting requirement.

atoms.semantic_claim: Enabling `reth-errors/async-context` forwards the feature to `reth-execution-errors`, exposing async-context behavior through this crate's public feature surface.

destinations.evidence: The destination crate has no `[features]` section, so the `reth-errors` package cannot expose `async-context` at all.
The downstream target of the passthrough already exists in the port, so only the `reth-errors` forwarding layer is missing.

The source repo has `async-context = ["reth-errors/async-context"]` in `crates/stages/api/Cargo.toml`, but the port dropped that feature too; this confirms the forwarding path is absent end-to-end in the port rather than replaced elsewhere.

atoms.equivalence_argument: No exact, renamed, upstream-covered, or dependency-moved equivalent exists for the `reth-errors` feature surface in the port. The dependency feature exists, but callers enabling `reth-errors/async-context` still fail because this crate does not re-export it.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run `cargo check --manifest-path /home/ritual/repos/ritual-reth-internal-v2.2.0-port/crates/errors/Cargo.toml --features async-context` and expect success. It currently fails with `the package `reth-errors` does not contain this feature: async-context`.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace the `reth-errors` async-context feature passthrough so downstream manifests can enable async-context through this crate boundary.
atoms.notes: High risk because missing feature propagation breaks async-context feature selection at the crate boundary and invalidates downstream manifests that rely on `reth-errors/async-context`.

summary: The port dropped the `reth-errors` async-context feature passthrough; the explicit empty default feature is redundant noise, but the missing passthrough is a real manifest regression.
notes: Verified that upstream v2.2.0 `crates/errors/Cargo.toml` also lacks this feature block, so the downstream feature was not absorbed upstream. The missing passthrough remains actionable in the port.
